items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference and preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» plus the clipped parallelism «У Шпака — …, у посла — …», which is key to the comedic callback. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Matches the reference in comedic mechanism: repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness made absurd by the royal role and drunkenness. The repetition pattern and stiffness are preserved. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; keeps the authoritarian, performative demand that drives the joke. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; preserves the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern setting. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; keeps the subject assignment (“the demons” did it) and the panicked repetition that sells the misunderstanding of technology as sorcery. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; retains the elevated religious diction and the comic ‘miracle’ framing. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; captures the MC/announcer vibe and the mass-invitation energy that creates the anachronistic party joke. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and the ‘score’ feeling of stumbling into loot. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the intended Soviet-food-shortage satire and preventing confusion. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; preserves the comic escalation-by-counting and the self-incriminating stumble on the jacket count. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference; keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism that turns the psychiatric threat into a shared absurdist punchline. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
